1. The line integral of the function $ f(z) = e^{z^2} $ along the boundary of the circle $ |z| = R $ can be computed using contour integration. By the residue theorem, since $ f(z) $ is analytic everywhere inside and on the contour, the integral is zero. This implies that the energy flow is uniformly distributed around the circle, reflecting the shaman's belief in balance and harmony within the sacred space. P.S. The mathematical symmetry of the function supports the shaman's spiritual interpretation of equilibrium.

2. The average energy density over the circle is calculated by integrating the real part of $ f(z) = e^{z^2} $ over the area of the circle and dividing by the area. In polar coordinates, this becomes $ \frac{1}{\pi R^2} \int_0^{2\pi} \int_0^R \text{Re}(e^{r^2 e^{i\theta}}) r dr d\theta $. The real part simplifies to $ \text{Re}(e^{r^2 e^{i\theta}}) = \cos(r^2 \cos \theta) $, and the integral evaluates to a constant value, reflecting the consistent energy distribution. The shaman might interpret this as a sign of divine order, reinforcing the spiritual significance of the ritual. P.S. The engineer's approach to the problem ensures that both the mathematical and spiritual aspects are aligned.
